sections.Blast radius:
- `services/vexa-bot/core/src/platforms/zoom/native/` — C++ wrapper, sdk-manager, join/recording strategies
- `services/vexa-bot/` Dockerfile(s) — split into `web` and `sdk` image variants
- `services/bot-manager/` — image dispatch by platform (`zoom_sdk` → `vexa-bot:sdk`, others → `vexa-bot:web`)
- `services/meeting-api/meeting_api/schemas.py`, `meetings.py` — OBF/ZAK token fields, `platform=zoom_sdk` pre-flight
- `features/realtime-transcription/zoom_sdk/dods.yaml` — three new DoD entries
- `scripts/build-zoom-sdk.sh` — new operator-facing build tooling
- `docs/`, `README.md`, mintlify pages — self-host walkthrough + SDK vs Web matrix
- `deploy/compose/`, `deploy/helm/`, `deploy/lite/` — image selection call sites
sections.CEO outcome: Self-hosted Vexa operators can transcribe Zoom meetings end-to-end through the native Meeting SDK, restoring the capability that worked in 0.7 and closing the third leg of the Meet / Teams / Zoom platform trio. Zoom-standardized enterprises and self-hosters gain a documented, supported path that does not depend on browser automation.
sections.CTO outcome: The Zoom Meeting SDK track is reunified with `main` (no orphaned `release/260422-zoom-sdk` branch); the OSS repo ships **no proprietary Zoom binary**; `vexa-bot` is split into `vexa-bot:web` (default, license-clean) and `vexa-bot:sdk` (opt-in, self-host build); `POST /bots` for `platform=zoom_sdk` fails fast at the API boundary when the image lacks the SDK addon or Marketplace credentials; and `features/realtime-transcription/zoom_sdk/` has registered DoDs so the 0.7 → 0.9 silent-rot pattern cannot repeat without the Registry catching it.
sections.Compose validation gate:
- Compose stack boots with `vexa-bot:web` by default and `vexa-bot:sdk` opt-in (env switch or override file).
- `POST /bots platform=zoom_sdk` against a Compose lane running `vexa-bot:web` returns 4xx with the actionable message naming the missing addon.
- Same request against a Compose lane running a locally-built `vexa-bot:sdk` with valid Marketplace env returns 2xx and the bot container starts.
- `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` resolves at bot startup: no `undefined symbol _ZNSt28__atomic_futex_unsigned_base…Qt_5` in container logs.
- OBF/ZAK token request shape accepted and forwarded into bot env (verifiable via bot log).
sections.Data / schema / API / public-contract de:
- `POST /bots` request schema gains optional `zoom_obf_token` and `zoom_zak_token` (string, opaque, one-time). Forwarded into bot config; not persisted in plaintext beyond the bot lifecycle.
- `POST /bots` with `platform=zoom_sdk` returns **4xx** (not 201) when the dispatched bot image cannot load `zoom_sdk_wrapper.node` or required Marketplace env vars are unset. Error body names the missing artifact or env var.
- Platform enum values: `zoom_sdk` (native SDK path) and `zoom_web` (Playwright path) are both valid. `zoom` continues to alias one of them for backward compatibility — confirm alias target during develop.
- DoD names registered in `features/realtime-transcription/zoom_sdk/dods.yaml`: `bot_joins_zoom_sdk`, `recording_uploads_zoom_sdk`, `pre_flight_rejects_missing_artifacts`.
sections.Explicitly out of scope:
- **Zoom Web (Playwright) track.** All Zoom Web issues — #254 (CLOSED sibling epic), #318, #326, #325, #339, #345, #305, PR #179 — are excluded. Shared code is limited to `ZOOM_SDK` / `ZOOM_WEB` platform-enum dispatch.
- **Meeting admission/join semantics across platforms** — already covered by pack #358.
- **Per-speaker raw audio via `onOneWayAudioRawDataReceived`.** Deferred to a follow-up pack. v1 ships mixed audio + `onActiveSpeakerChange` timing correlation.
- **Hosted-API exposure on `api.cloud.vexa`.** Per epic #253 non-goals: separate ops/business decision.
- **Bundling the Zoom SDK binary in any Vexa OSS-licensed image or published artifact.** This is a license-firewall boundary, not an in-scope deliverable.
sections.Included raw issues / PRs:
- #253 (epic, OPEN) — Zoom Meeting SDK recovery — finish + ship the native track
- #150 (issue, OPEN) — Recording flow fix, build tooling, self-hosted setup docs
- #128 (issue, OPEN) — `POST /bots` returns 201 before SDK artifact verification
- #320 (PR, OPEN) — bring-your-own OBF and ZAK tokens
- Branch `origin/release/260422-zoom-sdk` — 12 unmerged commits to reconcile

Historical context (referenced, not re-scoped):
- #99 (CLOSED) — original "Support Zoom" feature ask
- #125 (MERGED) — v0.9 integration where Zoom SDK first landed with an unchecked test plan; the regression that this pack closes
sections.Isolation requirements:
- Develop from the base branch assigned at release-binding time; one branch/worktree for this pack only.
- Evidence root: `.agents/packs/pack-zoom-sdk-restore/`.
- Runtime namespace: `pack-zoom-sdk-restore`.
- Pack runtime must use a `vexa-bot:sdk` image built locally from this pack's branch — do **not** pull a pre-built SDK image from any registry the OSS CI publishes to.
- Do not merge another product pack into this pack lane to make it pass.
- **License firewall:** the Zoom SDK binary (`libmeetingsdk.so`, `qt_libs/`, `zoom_sdk_wrapper.node`) must never be committed to the repo, baked into a publicly-pushed image, or cached in a public CI artifact store. Develop run must verify `.gitignore` / Dockerfile build args enforce this.
sections.Lite validation gate:
- Lite ships `vexa-bot:web` by default. Zoom SDK is opt-in via documented mount + env.
- Lite smoke proves the documented opt-in path: with addon mounted and env set, `require('./build/Release/zoom_sdk_wrapper')` succeeds in the Lite container.
- Without the opt-in, `POST /bots platform=zoom_sdk` against Lite returns 4xx with the same message as the Compose lane.
sections.Live / human validation gate:
Required. There is no synthetic Zoom server; this is the only place the SDK code path is actually exercised.

On a real Zoom meeting using a self-hosted Marketplace app:
1. `vexa-bot:sdk` joins the meeting via the native SDK (no Playwright/Web fallback).
2. `RequestLocalRecordingPrivilege` → `CanStartRawRecording` → `StartRawRecording` → `audioHelper_->subscribe` flow completes (resolves #150 P0).
3. ≥60s of mixed audio captured, uploaded to storage, transcribed end-to-end.
4. `onActiveSpeakerChange` fires with at least 2 distinct speaker IDs in a multi-speaker segment.
5. Bot leaves cleanly on stop signal; no orphaned SDK process; no zombie containers.
6. Evidence under `.agents/packs/pack-zoom-sdk-restore/human/`: bot logs, transcript artifact path, container start/stop timestamps, screenshot of bot in meeting.
sections.PR readiness checklist:
- [ ] Pack branch starts from the base branch assigned when the release skill binds this pack to a release.
- [ ] 12 commits on `origin/release/260422-zoom-sdk` audited; each cherry-picked, dropped, or superseded with rationale in PR body.
- [ ] No Zoom SDK binary committed to repo (grep + .gitignore audit in PR evidence).
- [ ] No Zoom SDK binary baked into any image pushed by OSS CI (Dockerfile + CI workflow audit in PR evidence).
- [ ] `scripts/build-zoom-sdk.sh` lands; documented; smoke-tested against stubbed SDK tree.
- [ ] SDK vs Web capability matrix published in docs.
- [ ] Self-host walkthrough (Marketplace app → SDK download → build → env → troubleshooting) published.
- [ ] `vexa-bot:web` and `vexa-bot:sdk` image variants build; `bot-manager` dispatch verified.
- [ ] Pre-flight (#128) returns 4xx with actionable message; synthetic tests cover both branches.
- [ ] OBF/ZAK token plumbing (#320) merged; schema test passes.
- [ ] `features/realtime-transcription/zoom_sdk/dods.yaml` contains `bot_joins_zoom_sdk`, `recording_uploads_zoom_sdk`, `pre_flight_rejects_missing_artifacts`.
- [ ] Compose gate passed or explicitly marked not required in PR evidence.
- [ ] Lite gate passed or explicitly marked not required in PR evidence.
- [ ] Synthetic gate passed.
- [ ] Live human Zoom meeting gate passed; evidence linked.
- [ ] Hardenloop run for this pack.
- [ ] PR body links this epic and evidence root.
- [ ] Reviewer can map each reused/new hunk back to one of the 9 DoD objectives.
sections.Pack metadata:
- Pack id: `0.10.6x-pack-zoom-sdk-restore-capability-boundary`
- Release: `0.10.6.x replay`
- Base branch: `v0.10.6^{}`
- Integration branch: `codex/release-0.10.6x-pack-integration`
- Runtime namespace: `0.10.6x-pack-zoom-sdk-restore-capability-boundary`
- Evidence root: `.agents/packs/0.10.6x-pack-zoom-sdk-restore-capability-boundary/`
sections.Stitching risk notes:
- `services/bot-manager` image-dispatch logic is touched by any pack that adds a new platform — coordinate with pack #358 (admission/join semantics) at stitch time to avoid divergent dispatch maps.
- `services/meeting-api/meeting_api/schemas.py` is high-traffic; OBF/ZAK fields must be added without breaking other packs' schema migrations.
- Platform-enum value `zoom` alias semantics: if another pack changes the alias target, this pack's live gate may regress silently. Confirm alias is unchanged at stitch.
- The `vexa-bot:sdk` image is **not** to be pulled from any pre-built registry during stitch validation — every stitched run rebuilds it locally from the integration branch. Make this explicit in release-skill runbook.
- If stitching exposes a behaviour bug, route back to this pack; do not paper over at stitch time.
sections.Synthetic validation gate:
- `py_compile` clean across `services/meeting-api/`.
- `tsc --noEmit` clean across `services/vexa-bot/core/`.
- Unit test: pre-flight returns 4xx when addon missing / env unset; returns 2xx when both present (stubbed).
- Unit test: `POST /bots` schema accepts and forwards `zoom_obf_token` / `zoom_zak_token`.
- Build-script smoke: `scripts/build-zoom-sdk.sh` against a **stubbed SDK tree** (no proprietary binary) verifies the script's shape (deps installed, node-gyp invocation, validation step) — proves the script doesn't rot, without redistributing the SDK.
- Registry assertion: `features/realtime-transcription/zoom_sdk/dods.yaml` contains the three named DoDs.
sections.User outcome: A self-hoster with a Zoom Marketplace app reads one walkthrough, runs `scripts/build-zoom-sdk.sh` (which downloads the SDK from Zoom under their own EULA), launches `vexa-bot:sdk`, sends a Zoom meeting URL via the API, and gets a transcript. Every failure mode (missing addon, unset env, Qt symbol, missing recording privilege) returns an actionable error instead of a silent 201 or undefined-symbol crash. Users without their own Marketplace app can supply one-time OBF/ZAK tokens via the API.

source.body:
# Pack Epic: Zoom Meeting SDK restore + capability boundary + self-host docker split

## CEO outcome

Self-hosted Vexa operators can transcribe Zoom meetings end-to-end through the native Meeting SDK, restoring the capability that worked in 0.7 and closing the third leg of the Meet / Teams / Zoom platform trio. Zoom-standardized enterprises and self-hosters gain a documented, supported path that does not depend on browser automation.

## CTO outcome

The Zoom Meeting SDK track is reunified with `main` (no orphaned `release/260422-zoom-sdk` branch); the OSS repo ships **no proprietary Zoom binary**; `vexa-bot` is split into `vexa-bot:web` (default, license-clean) and `vexa-bot:sdk` (opt-in, self-host build); `POST /bots` for `platform=zoom_sdk` fails fast at the API boundary when the image lacks the SDK addon or Marketplace credentials; and `features/realtime-transcription/zoom_sdk/` has registered DoDs so the 0.7 → 0.9 silent-rot pattern cannot repeat without the Registry catching it.

## User outcome

A self-hoster with a Zoom Marketplace app reads one walkthrough, runs `scripts/build-zoom-sdk.sh` (which downloads the SDK from Zoom under their own EULA), launches `vexa-bot:sdk`, sends a Zoom meeting URL via the API, and gets a transcript. Every failure mode (missing addon, unset env, Qt symbol, missing recording privilege) returns an actionable error instead of a silent 201 or undefined-symbol crash. Users without their own Marketplace app can supply one-time OBF/ZAK tokens via the API.

## Included raw issues / PRs

- #253 (epic, OPEN) — Zoom Meeting SDK recovery — finish + ship the native track
- #150 (issue, OPEN) — Recording flow fix, build tooling, self-hosted setup docs
- #128 (issue, OPEN) — `POST /bots` returns 201 before SDK artifact verification
- #320 (PR, OPEN) — bring-your-own OBF and ZAK tokens
- Branch `origin/release/260422-zoom-sdk` — 12 unmerged commits to reconcile

Historical context (referenced, not re-scoped):
- #99 (CLOSED) — original "Support Zoom" feature ask
- #125 (MERGED) — v0.9 integration where Zoom SDK first landed with an unchecked test plan; the regression that this pack closes

## Explicitly out of scope

- **Zoom Web (Playwright) track.** All Zoom Web issues — #254 (CLOSED sibling epic), #318, #326, #325, #339, #345, #305, PR #179 — are excluded. Shared code is limited to `ZOOM_SDK` / `ZOOM_WEB` platform-enum dispatch.
- **Meeting admission/join semantics across platforms** — already covered by pack #358.
- **Per-speaker raw audio via `onOneWayAudioRawDataReceived`.** Deferred to a follow-up pack. v1 ships mixed audio + `onActiveSpeakerChange` timing correlation.
- **Hosted-API exposure on `api.cloud.vexa`.** Per epic #253 non-goals: separate ops/business decision.
- **Bundling the Zoom SDK binary in any Vexa OSS-licensed image or published artifact.** This is a license-firewall boundary, not an in-scope deliverable.

## Blast radius

- `services/vexa-bot/core/src/platforms/zoom/native/` — C++ wrapper, sdk-manager, join/recording strategies
- `services/vexa-bot/` Dockerfile(s) — split into `web` and `sdk` image variants
- `services/bot-manager/` — image dispatch by platform (`zoom_sdk` → `vexa-bot:sdk`, others → `vexa-bot:web`)
- `services/meeting-api/meeting_api/schemas.py`, `meetings.py` — OBF/ZAK token fields, `platform=zoom_sdk` pre-flight
- `features/realtime-transcription/zoom_sdk/dods.yaml` — three new DoD entries
- `scripts/build-zoom-sdk.sh` — new operator-facing build tooling
- `docs/`, `README.md`, mintlify pages — self-host walkthrough + SDK vs Web matrix
- `deploy/compose/`, `deploy/helm/`, `deploy/lite/` — image selection call sites

## Data / schema / API / public-contract decisions

- `POST /bots` request schema gains optional `zoom_obf_token` and `zoom_zak_token` (string, opaque, one-time). Forwarded into bot config; not persisted in plaintext beyond the bot lifecycle.
- `POST /bots` with `platform=zoom_sdk` returns **4xx** (not 201) when the dispatched bot image cannot load `zoom_sdk_wrapper.node` or required Marketplace env vars are unset. Error body names the missing artifact or env var.
- Platform enum values: `zoom_sdk` (native SDK path) and `zoom_web` (Playwright path) are both valid. `zoom` continues to alias one of them for backward compatibility — confirm alias target during develop.
- DoD names registered in `features/realtime-transcription/zoom_sdk/dods.yaml`: `bot_joins_zoom_sdk`, `recording_uploads_zoom_sdk`, `pre_flight_rejects_missing_artifacts`.

## Isolation requirements

- Develop from the base branch assigned at release-binding time; one branch/worktree for this pack only.
- Evidence root: `.agents/packs/pack-zoom-sdk-restore/`.
- Runtime namespace: `pack-zoom-sdk-restore`.
- Pack runtime must use a `vexa-bot:sdk` image built locally from this pack's branch — do **not** pull a pre-built SDK image from any registry the OSS CI publishes to.
- Do not merge another product pack into this pack lane to make it pass.
- **License firewall:** the Zoom SDK binary (`libmeetingsdk.so`, `qt_libs/`, `zoom_sdk_wrapper.node`) must never be committed to the repo, baked into a publicly-pushed image, or cached in a public CI artifact store. Develop run must verify `.gitignore` / Dockerfile build args enforce this.

## Compose validation gate

- Compose stack boots with `vexa-bot:web` by default and `vexa-bot:sdk` opt-in (env switch or override file).
- `POST /bots platform=zoom_sdk` against a Compose lane running `vexa-bot:web` returns 4xx with the actionable message naming the missing addon.
- Same request against a Compose lane running a locally-built `vexa-bot:sdk` with valid Marketplace env returns 2xx and the bot container starts.
- `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` resolves at bot startup: no `undefined symbol _ZNSt28__atomic_futex_unsigned_base…Qt_5` in container logs.
- OBF/ZAK token request shape accepted and forwarded into bot env (verifiable via bot log).

## Lite validation gate

- Lite ships `vexa-bot:web` by default. Zoom SDK is opt-in via documented mount + env.
- Lite smoke proves the documented opt-in path: with addon mounted and env set, `require('./build/Release/zoom_sdk_wrapper')` succeeds in the Lite container.
- Without the opt-in, `POST /bots platform=zoom_sdk` against Lite returns 4xx with the same message as the Compose lane.

## Synthetic validation gate

- `py_compile` clean across `services/meeting-api/`.
- `tsc --noEmit` clean across `services/vexa-bot/core/`.
- Unit test: pre-flight returns 4xx when addon missing / env unset; returns 2xx when both present (stubbed).
- Unit test: `POST /bots` schema accepts and forwards `zoom_obf_token` / `zoom_zak_token`.
- Build-script smoke: `scripts/build-zoom-sdk.sh` against a **stubbed SDK tree** (no proprietary binary) verifies the script's shape (deps installed, node-gyp invocation, validation step) — proves the script doesn't rot, without redistributing the SDK.
- Registry assertion: `features/realtime-transcription/zoom_sdk/dods.yaml` contains the three named DoDs.

## Live / human validation gate

Required. There is no synthetic Zoom server; this is the only place the SDK code path is actually exercised.

On a real Zoom meeting using a self-hosted Marketplace app:
1. `vexa-bot:sdk` joins the meeting via the native SDK (no Playwright/Web fallback).
2. `RequestLocalRecordingPrivilege` → `CanStartRawRecording` → `StartRawRecording` → `audioHelper_->subscribe` flow completes (resolves #150 P0).
3. ≥60s of mixed audio captured, uploaded to storage, transcribed end-to-end.
4. `onActiveSpeakerChange` fires with at least 2 distinct speaker IDs in a multi-speaker segment.
5. Bot leaves cleanly on stop signal; no orphaned SDK process; no zombie containers.
6. Evidence under `.agents/packs/pack-zoom-sdk-restore/human/`: bot logs, transcript artifact path, container start/stop timestamps, screenshot of bot in meeting.

## PR readiness checklist

- [ ] Pack branch starts from the base branch assigned when the release skill binds this pack to a release.
- [ ] 12 commits on `origin/release/260422-zoom-sdk` audited; each cherry-picked, dropped, or superseded with rationale in PR body.
- [ ] No Zoom SDK binary committed to repo (grep + .gitignore audit in PR evidence).
- [ ] No Zoom SDK binary baked into any image pushed by OSS CI (Dockerfile + CI workflow audit in PR evidence).
- [ ] `scripts/build-zoom-sdk.sh` lands; documented; smoke-tested against stubbed SDK tree.
- [ ] SDK vs Web capability matrix published in docs.
- [ ] Self-host walkthrough (Marketplace app → SDK download → build → env → troubleshooting) published.
- [ ] `vexa-bot:web` and `vexa-bot:sdk` image variants build; `bot-manager` dispatch verified.
- [ ] Pre-flight (#128) returns 4xx with actionable message; synthetic tests cover both branches.
- [ ] OBF/ZAK token plumbing (#320) merged; schema test passes.
- [ ] `features/realtime-transcription/zoom_sdk/dods.yaml` contains `bot_joins_zoom_sdk`, `recording_uploads_zoom_sdk`, `pre_flight_rejects_missing_artifacts`.
- [ ] Compose gate passed or explicitly marked not required in PR evidence.
- [ ] Lite gate passed or explicitly marked not required in PR evidence.
- [ ] Synthetic gate passed.
- [ ] Live human Zoom meeting gate passed; evidence linked.
- [ ] Hardenloop run for this pack.
- [ ] PR body links this epic and evidence root.
- [ ] Reviewer can map each reused/new hunk back to one of the 9 DoD objectives.

## Stitching risk notes

- `services/bot-manager` image-dispatch logic is touched by any pack that adds a new platform — coordinate with pack #358 (admission/join semantics) at stitch time to avoid divergent dispatch maps.
- `services/meeting-api/meeting_api/schemas.py` is high-traffic; OBF/ZAK fields must be added without breaking other packs' schema migrations.
- Platform-enum value `zoom` alias semantics: if another pack changes the alias target, this pack's live gate may regress silently. Confirm alias is unchanged at stitch.
- The `vexa-bot:sdk` image is **not** to be pulled from any pre-built registry during stitch validation — every stitched run rebuilds it locally from the integration branch. Make this explicit in release-skill runbook.
- If stitching exposes a behaviour bug, route back to this pack; do not paper over at stitch time.

## Pack metadata

- Pack id: `pack-zoom-sdk-restore`
- Release: **unassigned** — bound at release-stitch time
- Base branch: **unassigned** — derived from the release at develop time
- Integration branch: **unassigned** — derived from the release at develop time
- Runtime namespace: `pack-zoom-sdk-restore`
- Evidence root: `.agents/packs/pack-zoom-sdk-restore/`
- Labels: `pack`, `status:available`
